content: (The location is now Fort Amol.) (Characters nearby (from nearest to furthest): delphine, Jenassa, Lydia, Goat, Fox) (MJR: k101DaegonFollowerHelloLines; Daegon: Uh huh? MJR: I have some rules to set.; Daegon: I won't play nice, even if you ask nice. MJR: k101DaegonFollowerHelloLines; Daegon: There's my little pet. MJR: Any thoughts?; Daegon: I like to collect souls. At night, I like to take them out of their box and place with them like dolls.. MJR: k101DaegonFollowerHelloLines; Daegon: Yes sir? MJR: Hey, Princess....; Daegon: Yes...)

Daegon: This Fort Amol looks like it's seen better days—crumbling walls under that endless starry sky. Reminds me of the ruins back in Summerset, where I'd sneak off to practice spells without the priests watching. You ever wonder what ghosts haunt places like this at night? Farmer: Aye, ghosts and worse, lass. Been tilling fields near here for years, and the nights get eerie with those ancient stones whispering secrets. That clear sky's a blessing after the rain we had earlier—keeps the mud off my boots, at least.

(*You are observing the following:* In this nighttime scene, a vast, ink-black sky dominates the view, twinkling with countless faint stars scattered across its expanse, evoking a sense of remote wilderness. Tall, dense evergreen trees with dark, needle-covered branches frame the edges, their silhouettes rising sharply against the starry backdrop, some leaning slightly as if caught in a gentle breeze. In the foreground, rugged gray rocks jut out from the earth, partially shrouded in shadow and surrounded by clusters of low shrubs and patches of sparse grass. A winding dirt path stretches ahead, its uneven surface dusted with fine soil and scattered with small pebbles, leading deeper into the shadowy, forested terrain under the clear, moonless night.)
